Article_Title: 167_Optimizing HMI for Intelligent Electric Vehicles Using BCI and Deep Neural Networks with Genetic Algorithms
Reference_Text_Preview: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic alg...
Full_Reference_Text: This study utilizes a brain—computer interface (BCI)—based deep neural network (DNN) and genetic algorithm (GA) method. The deep neural network (DNN) topology linking HMI visual coding to e-Sense prediction comprises three layers: an input layer with 26 neurons, three fully connected hidden layers, and an output layer with one neurone. The network utilized rectified linear unit (ReLU) activation functions across the hidden layers, with a regression layer at the output. Deep neural networks (DNNs) play a key role in the personalized human-machine interaction interfaces (HMIs) of intelligent electric vehicles. By analyzing complex user data, interactions are customized, the learning environment is optimized, and the user experience is enhanced.
